meta.title: How to reduce your Box 3 tax: 5 tips that actually work
meta.description: Five legal ways to pay less Box 3 tax in 2025. Fiscal partnership, green investments, reference-date optimisation and more.
meta.keywords: reduce box 3 tax, box 3 tips, pay less box 3, green investment exemption, fiscal partner box 3, box 3 optimise

hero.title: How to reduce your Box 3 tax
hero.subtitle: There is no magic trick. But there are choices you can make, and they do make a difference.

tips.title: Make the most of your fiscal partnership
body: If you have a fiscal partner, you may divide your Box 3 assets between you in whichever way is most tax-efficient. In practice that means: you can shift assets from the partner with the higher taxable income to the other.

On top of that, the tax-free allowance applies per person. Together you are entitled to €114,000 in exemption rather than €57,000. On an average-sized portfolio that quickly saves hundreds of euros a year.
The split does not have to be fifty-fifty. You are free to optimise, but do it through your tax return, not after the fact.

tips.title: Pay off debts before 1 January
body: Box 3 is calculated on the reference date of 1 January. Your net asset position on that day determines your assessment for the whole year.

If you can make a significant repayment before year-end and thereby lower your Box 3 base, it can sometimes be worth doing. Note: debts are only deductible above a threshold of €3,700 (or €7,400 with a partner). Paying off small debts therefore has no tax effect.
This is a timing consideration, not a workaround. It reduces your actual assets, but it also reduces your tax bill.

tips.title: Invest (partly) in green funds
body: Green investments are exempt from Box 3 up to €71,251 per person (up to €142,502 with a partner). If you are planning to invest anyway and have a choice, it is worth checking whether sustainable alternatives work for you.
This applies to investments in funds carrying the Dutch tax authority's quality mark. Not every fund that calls itself "green" qualifies. Check with your bank or broker.
The exemption directly reduces your tax base, meaning you pay tax on a smaller portion of your assets.

tips.title: Check your tax return carefully
body: This sounds obvious, but errors are common. The tax authority pre-fills part of the return based on bank balances and property valuations, but that information is not always correct.
At minimum check: are all debts above the threshold included? Is the WOZ value of your second property correct? Are your green investments declared as such?
Use this calculator to have a reference point before filing your return. If the outcome differs significantly from what the tax authority calculates, that is a reason to investigate further.

tips.title: Follow the legal developments
body: Legal proceedings about Box 3 are still ongoing. People who overpaid in earlier years under the old system may in some cases be entitled to remediation or a refund.

Whether you are entitled to anything depends on whether you filed a timely objection and which year is involved. The Supreme Court and lower courts continue to hand down rulings, sometimes with consequences for your situation.
It is worth looking into, especially if you had substantial assets in the years 2017 to 2022. Consult a tax advisor if you think you may be entitled to remediation.

disclaimerText: if you have assets above the tax-free threshold, you cannot escape Box 3 tax entirely. The tips above help you limit it, but they do not eliminate it. For more complex situations (large property portfolios, multiple investment accounts, ongoing objection procedures) a tax advisor is worth the cost.
